original: Teotihuacan General Siyaj K'ak', conquers the important Mayan city Tikal and deposes its ruler
Formal approval of the Order of Templars at the Council of Troyes
Arnošt of Pardubice becomes the first Archbishop of Prague (and the last bishop of Prague)
Church authorities in Rome burn Hebrew books
1st Connecticut constitution (Fundamental Orders) adopted in Hartford, published by Rodger Ludlow
Dutch East India Company conquers the city of Malacca, 7,000 killed
Battle at Elvas: Portuguese beat Spanish
Portuguese trading port of Bombay is officially transferred to English control, as part of the dowry of Portuguese princess Catherine of Braganza
The musical instrument, the clarinet is invented in Nürnberg, Germany
Massachusetts holds day of fasting for wrongly persecuting "witches"
German mob leader and thief "Sjako" sentenced to death in Amsterdam
Spanish King Philip V abdicates throne, in favor of his son 17 year old son Louis; returns to rule in September when Louis dies of smallbox
Britain and Spain sign 2nd Convention of Pardo - seeks to establish boundary between Georgia and Florida
US Congress of the Confederation ratifies the Treaty of Paris, signed September 3, 1783, ending the Revolutionary War, and forwards it to Great Britain
Dr Jesse Bennett performs 1st successful Cesarean section operation in the US, on his wife, Elizabeth, delivering a baby girl at theie home in Edom, Virginia
King of Naples flees before advancing French armies
Gideon Hawley becomes 1st state school superintendent in US (NY)
Treaty of Kiel: King Frederick VI of Denmark cedes the Kingdom of Norway to King Charles XIII of Sweden
Greek War of Independence: Acrocorinth is captured by Theodoros Kolokotronis and Demetrius Ypsilanti
Battle between gunboats at Bayou Teche, Louisiana
Skirmish at Cosby Creek, Tennessee - General Robert B. Vance captured
North Carolina constitutional convention meets in Raleigh
South Carolina constitutional convention meets with a black majority
I. D. Shadd elected Speaker of lower house of Mississippi legislature
US Supreme court rules race separation on trains unconstitutional
6,960 m (22,834 ft) Cerro Aconcagua (Argentina) 1st climbed by European expedition led by British mountaineer Edward FitzGerald
Australian cricketer Joe Darling hits the 1st six in Tests (out of the ground)
An earthquake in Kingston, Jamaica, kills more than 1,000
Roger Hartigan scores century on Test debut v England in Adelaide (116)
Dutch Zuiderzee dyke cracks
Finland and USSR adopt New Style (Gregorian) calendar
John McGraw, Charles A. Stoneham and Francis Xavier McQuade buy NY Giants
Toronto Maple Leafs 1st hat trick (Hap Day) vs NY Rangers
Afghan King Amanullah forced to resign
1st totalisator (to record racetrack bets) in US is installed Hialeah Park Race Track in Hialeah, Florida
Iraq-Mediterranean oil pipeline goes into use
National Society for Legalization of Euthanasia forms (NY)
All commercial ferry services between San Francisco and East Bay end
Norway claims Queen Maud Land in Antarctica
Japanese troops land at oil center Balikpapan in Borneo
Montreal Canadiens' left wing Alex Smart becomes the first NHL rookie to score a hat trick in his first NHL game, a 5-1 win at home over the Chicago Black Hawks
World War II: Japan begins Operation Ke, withdrawal of its troops from Guadalcanal
Soviet army begins offensive at Oranienbaum/Wolchow
In Greece, Communists and the British agree to a cease-fire in the struggle to control Athens (and with it Greece)
"Sanford & Son" starring Redd Foxx and Demond Wilson premieres on NBC TV
WMAH TV channel 19 in Biloxi, Mississippi (PBS) begins broadcasting
WMAU TV channel 17 in Bude, Mississippi (PBS) begins broadcasting
WMAW TV channel 14 in Meridian, Mississippi (PBS) begins broadcasting
2 Royal Ulster Constabulary officers are killed in Derry, Northern Ireland by a booby-trap bomb attached to their car by the Irish Republican Army
Dancer Roy Castle is measured at 1,440 taps/min on BBC TV
Grateful Dead bass player Phil Lesh busted on drug possession charges at his Marin County, California home
Super Bowl VII, LA Memorial Coliseum, Los Angeles, CA: Miami Dolphins beat Washington Redskins, 14-7; MVP: Jake Scott, Miami, S
World Football League founded
17-year-old Lesley Whittle is kidnapped in Shropshire, England
Anita Wold (Norway) sets women's ski jump distance record-98 m
USSR breaks trade agreement with US
"The Bionic Woman" with Lindsay Wagner debuts on ABC (later NBC)
"Fantasy Island" starring Ricardo Montalbán and Hervé Villechaize premieres on ABC TV
RCA releasesDavid Bowie's 11th studio album, "Low"; the album is the first collaboration with Brian Eno, in what becomes known as his 'Berlin trilogy'
US Federal Communications Commission frees stations to air as many commercials an hour as they wish
16 indicted by US for granting sanctuary to Central American refugees
British pound sinks to record low $US1.11
Constitution of Guatemala takes effect
Vinicio Cerezo becomes only the second freely elected President of Guatemala since CIA-sponsored coup in 1954
Pitcher Jim "Catfish" Hunter and outfielder Billy Williams are elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
29-year-old French woman Marie Claude Adam gives birth to sextuplets in Paris
Former Belgian premier Paul Vanden Boeynants kidnapped
"Barbara DeAngelis Show" premieres on CBS-TV
Jorge Serrano Elias sworn in as President of Guatemala
Tyne Daly arrested for drunk driving in Van Nuys, California
Valentin Pavlov become new premier of USSR
Polish ferry boat capsizes in storm, 50 die
Inna Lassovskaya triple-jumps ladies world record (14.61m)
Russian manned space craft TM-17, lands
The Duchess of Kent converts to Catholicism, the first member of the Royal Family to do so in more than 300 years
10,000 South Africans attend state funeral of Joe Slovo
100th episode of "Ellen" airs
Researchers in Dallas, Texas present findings about an enzyme that slows aging and cell death (apoptosis)
Mel Lastman, Mayor of Toronto, calls in the Army to help with snow removal and emergency medical evacuations after major snowstorm hits the city
A United Nations tribunal sentences five Bosnian Croats to up to 25 years for the 1993 killing of over 100 Muslims in a Bosnian village.
UK declared free of foot-and-mouth cattle disease
The national flag of Georgia, the so-called "five cross flag" restored to official use after a hiatus of some 500 years
Landing of the Huygens probe on Saturn's moon Titan
MESSENGER spacecraft performs a Mercury flyby
Yemen declares war on al-Qaeda
Stampede near Sabarimala in Kerala, India kills 104 devotees and injures 100 more
Tunisian president Ben Ali, flees to Saudi Arabia after popular protests known as the Jasmine Revolution
Ma Ying-jeou wins re-election as President of the Republic of China with 51% of the vote
Suicide bomber kills 53 and injures 130 in Basra, Iraq
American journalist David Satter is expelled from Russia
63 people are killed and 70,000 are left homeless after floods devastate Mozambique and Malawi
Sixth republican presidential candidates debate hosted by Fox, held in North Charleston, South Carolina
Americans chance of dying from an accidental opioid overdose higher than a car accident for the first time, according to US National Safety Council
China posts shock drop in trade figures with a 4.4% fall in exports for December and a 7.6% fall in imports
Pawel Adamowicz, Mayor of Gdańsk, Poland, stabbed on stage at charity event. Dies two days later.
Spinning disk of ice 300m wide, resembling the moon, forms in the Presumpscot river, Westbrook, Maine
US Republican leaders strip Congressman Steve King from House committees after series of racist comments
Boston Red Sox manager Alex Cora is fired amid Major League Baseball's investigation into sign-stealing by the Houston Astros; Cora was the Astros' bench coach before moving to Boston
Brazilian governor of Amazonas state Wilson Lima says their COVID-19 surge is now as bad as April 2020, with hospitals running out of oxygen and patients ventilated manually
Former Michigan Gov. Rick Snyder charged and pleads not guilty to two counts of willful neglect of duty over the Flint water scandal that killed 12 people
Dan Campbellleads the Detroit Lions to their first playoff win since 1991, defeating the Los Angeles Rams
Frederik X becomes King of Denmark at Christiansborg Castle, Copenhagen, after the abdication of his mother Margrethe at 83, and the world's last reigning queen

US Justice Department releases special counsel Jack Smith's final report on former and future presidentDonald Trump's efforts to overturn the 2020 election; Trump responds on social media calling Smith "deranged" and "a lamebrain"
Martin Lutherenters the University of Erfurt, aged 17
Charles VandFrancis Isign the Treaty of Madrid, forcing Francis to give up claims to Burgundy, Italy and Flanders
"Bonnie Prince Charlie", PrinceCharles Edward Stuart's Jacobite army leaves Glasgow, Scotland [OS=Jan 3]
WolfgangMozartcompletes his String Quartet No. 19 in C ("Dissonance quartet"), the last of six quartet set dedicated toJoseph Haydn(Op. 10/6, K. 465)
American inventorEli Whitneyreceives government contract for 10,000 muskets
French EmperorNapoleon IIIescapes attempt on his life by Felice Orsini, an Italian patriot who is later executed
"Celluloid" registered as a trademark by its inventorJohn Wesley Hyatt
British Prince Albert Victor, oldest son of the Prince of Wales (laterEdward VII) dies during a pandemic, his brother eventually becomes George V
Giacomo Puccini's opera "Tosca", with libretto by  Luigi Illica and Giuseppe Giacosa premieres the Teatro Costanzi in Rome, Italy
Roald Amundsen's South Pole expedition lands on the Ross Ice Shelf
Raymond Poincarébecomes Prime Minister of France for the first time
TheGandhi-Smuts Agreement is reached between GeneralJan SmutsandMahatma Gandhi, regarding voluntary registration, poll tax, recognition of Indian marriages and other matters in South Africa
Racing Hall of Fame jockeyEddie Arcarowins his 1st race at Agua Caliente racetrack in Tijuana, Mexico - aged 16 years
L. M. (Mario) Giannini elected President ofBank of America
MLB CommissionerKenesaw Mountain Landisgives free agency to 91 Detroit Tigers minor league players
World War II:Franklin D. Roosevelttravels from Miami to Morocco to meet withWinston Churchill, becoming the first American president to travel overseas by airplane
Yugoslavia elects its 1st president, MarshalTito
American rockerLittle Richardreleases single "Tutti Frutti"
"The Bell Jar" bySylvia Plathis published by Heinemann in the UK, the author commits suicide a month later
George Wallacesworn in as Governor of Alabama, his address states "segregation now; segregation tomorrow; segregation forever!"
Jacqueline Kennedy's 1st public appearance (TV) since US PresidentJFK's assassination
David Bowiereleases his 1st single "Can't Help Thinking About Me"
Sonny &Cherrelease single "The Beat Goes On"
Super Bowl II, Miami Orange Bowl, Miami, FL: Green Bay Packers beat Oakland Raiders, 33-14; MVP: Bart Starr, Green Bay, QB
Margrethe IIof Denmark becomes the first Queen of Denmark since 1412
"Aloha from Hawaii via Satellite", anElvis Presleyconcert broadcast live to Asia and Oceana, and on delay to Europe and US, becomes the most watched broadcast by an individual entertainer
Ted Turnerbecomes CEO of Atlanta Braves
John McEnroewins his first of 3 season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix tennis titles 6–7, 6–3, 7–5 against fellow AmericanArthur Asheat Madison Square Garden, NYC
Puerto Rican boxer Wilfred Benitez beats WBC welterweight champion Carlos Palomino of Mexico in a 15-round split decision in San Juan, Puerto Rico; becomes two-division champion at the age of 20
US PresidentJimmy CarterproposesMartin Luther King Jr.'s birthday be a federal holiday
Madonnadebuts her single "Holiday" on American Bandstand televiison program
Ray Manciniscores a 3rd-round TKO of Bobby Chacon to retain his WBA lightweight title in Reno, Nevada; Chacon so severely battered he thanks referee Richard Steele for stopping the fight
Martina Navratilovais 3rd to win 100 tennis tournaments (Connors & Evert)
1,000 muslims burn Salman Rushdies' "Satanic Verses" in Bradford, England
Un Secretary-General Javier Perez de Cuellar says he has lost all hope for peace in Gulf
David Lettermanannounces his show is moving from NBC to CBS
Utah Jazz center Mark Eaton blocks 2 shots in a 96-89 win over Seattle SuperSonics; becomes only 2nd player in NBA history (along withKareem Abdul-Jabbar) to record 3,000 career blocks

Mexico pledges profits from state-owned Pemex's $7-billion-per-year oil revenues in an effort to secure US congressional approval of loan guarantees;President Clintonapproves a $20-billion U.S. aid package for Mexico

Vinny Pazienza beats Panamanian boxing legendRoberto Duránin a 12-round unanimous decision in a super middleweight bout in Atlantic City; 2nd time in 6 months Pazienza wears down 43-year-old Duran in a high profile pay-pay-view bout
Charles Barkleypleads not guilty to an assault charge
Jennifer Lopezscores her first UK No.1 single with 'Love Don't Cost A Thing'
Legendary Russian test pilotMarina Popovichis awarded the honorary title "The Hero of the Nation" by the Russian Federation
Mike Pencesworn in as 50th governor of Indiana
Blue Note Records releases singer-songwriterRosanne Cash's critically acclaimed thirteenth album "The River & The Thread"; produced by her husband John Leventhal, it wins 3 Grammy Awards
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's 18th studio album "High Hopes", produced with members of the E Street Band, including the deceased Danny Federici and Clarence Clemons

US PresidentDonald Trumpdenies he is a Russian agent after NY Times article states the FBI started an investigation and the Washington Post raised issues over a meeting with Russian PresidentVladimir Putin
US game show Jeopardy "greatest of all time" tournament won byKen Jennings
Pope Francis, 84, and Pope EmeritusBenedict XVIreceive their first dose of a COVID-19 vaccine
Uganda elections re-elect President Museveni (in power since 1986), with main opposition presidential candidateBobi Winedisputing the result
US Secret Service takes control ofJoe Biden's inauguration as 20,000 troops authorized to guard Washington D.C., more than those stationed in Afghanistan, Iraq, Syria and Somalia
Brock Purdywins his first playoff game as a rookie quarterback against the Seattle Seahawks

Third Battle of Panipat: In one of the largest battles of the century, the mostly Muslim Afghani Durrani Empire defeats the mostly Hindu Maratha Empire in Northern India. An estimated 60,000–70,000 were killed in the fighting and about 40,000 Maratha prisoners massacred afterwards.
World War II: Casablanca Conference begins between Winston Churchill,Franklin D. Roosevelt, and other Allied representatives
US Army promotesElvis Presleyto Sergeant
